text: is the Central Beach of Paradise a celestial place? A macrocosmic reality, up there, literally, in the Pleiades, in mid-heaven? Or was it a beautiful memory of Tulum thirty years before, when Sanna was genuinely awakened, right here, and leading dance ceremonies, during the psychedelic 1970s? Or was it only the temples of the mind? Paradoxically, it was all the above. I could now feel Sanna’s spirit as authentically as Tulum, she was enlightened on the shores of Tulum, and this permeated her essence, her sunny spirit, that mercurial soul. Hermes Trismegistus of Hellenistic Egypt refers to the cycle of precession as “the Great Year of the Pleiades.” The Hindus saw the Pleiades as Agni—the God of Fire. 1 The First Fire Ceremony was practiced by Toltec and Mayan priests. The Pleiades were culminating with the sun on the zenith; the phrase “Playo Ides” rolled over and over in my head, Ides —as in the Ides of March —but I pronounced it like the eddies on the beach. It translated the central shore , and this implied the fissure of commissure conjunct the corpus callosum between the temples of the skull, as well as the biocosmic temples (the pyramids) above, and below, in the fractal world of the liminal vision. The central sun in the Pleiades is Alcyone. The homonym of Alcyone is “all cyan,” which calls to mind the Blue Star of Hopi myth. My heart was reflecting the light of the sun on the zenith back up into my skull, illuminating the mind’s eye. I thought I recognized the constellation Gemini as the Hero Twins from the Popol Vuh. I saw them as the personifications of the Ida and Pingala , connecting the root and crown chakras, with the emotional tones of tragedy and comedy. I recognized them to be the guardians of the serpent ladder, which connected Xi Balba Be, at the galactic center, with the Pleiades, culminating on the zenith. The cosmic portal was opened outside by the sun and moon and the constellations. It was opened inside by the liminal figures from the otherworld; they performed the ritual choreography to execute the hypnogogic journey. It was sanctioned through the Lifting the Shadow ceremony. And it was negotiated with the heart-mind techniques of nonaggression toward the self and others. All the players in the vision corresponded with a stellar group. For example, the jaguar as Leo; the guards as Gemini; the serpent as Serpens; the dolphins as Dorado, the dolphin fish. Each constellation depicts an archetype, or great force in the spirit, corresponding to one of the figures in the vision. Leo, as the Sphinx, initiated the trance. Gemini, as two universal forces holding tension, producing current. Serpens Cauda touching galactic center. Dorado, the Dolphin Fish, the south precessional pole. They are mercurial and alchemical signs. Knowing something about them and their symbolic milieu can open a dialogue, offering some insights when they appear in meditation, or out there in the world of appearances. They are transformative symbols of alchemical processes; they exist independent of our ego, on other planes, where aspects of our being coexist elsewhere. The star patterns outside the body are closely associated with a set of myths concerning something going on inside the body-mind. Constellations are signs regulating the human condition. Mythologies are like operating instructions, written by our ancestors, for the likes of us. Myths illuminate the (mostly) invisible and unconscious world, including the liminal and imaginal realms, expressing truth in the language of the right brain—using symbols. Myths, like walnuts, require cracking, they require memorizing, they need a cogitation period, and finally they reveal their insights (it’s as though insight has a literal meaning in the otherworld). It follows, then, that the constellations are like the picture book (think right-brain version) of the operating instructions. Constellations were discovered by our earliest forebearers, before the advent of writing; they’re like messages in a bottle; they are practically eternal patterns. And they serve many species, not only humans. Humans mythologized them. The human condition occupies a mysterious position in the known universe; we are right in the middle of everything, a multifaceted fractal whole, in two parts. Spontaneously, the vision resumed: I was inside the biocosmic temple. The same pyramid that I’d seen from the outside, but I was inside now. It was composed of geometric tiers of glowing spheres, exquisitely arranged in concentric rings or squares. Between the spheres were deep geometric nests, marked by more glowing orbs. I study the patterns, trying to memorize them. While I was concentrating, I became aware of a vibration that rose and fell, like the tide; it rode up and lay back down. It went into a chant: “Pull-lay-at-ease.” Play-at-ease. Pleiades! It was a droning sound, buzzing like a bee, while I slowly rematerialized back into my fully conscious body. My whole being felt like I was vibrating at a very natural frequency, perfectly “attuned” with the ocean, in tune with the sky, in tune with electron shells of love in all directions, fully at peace, deeply at ease. My heart of stone now felt like the sun shining inside. Relief is corporeal. The Lifting the Shadow ceremony succeeded in lifting my shadow. It’s clear to me now that Sanna’s soul and spirit have achieved their sublime destinations: her soul’s back home in heaven; and she’s free to dance in Paradise. Sanna was a radical emissary of light. She returned to the biocosmic temple, a beloved celebrity now. This vision in Tulum was a transpersonal experience. It was much more than a daydream. It felt like I’d discovered a lost continent beneath the sea; an otherworld, previously hidden from view. I believe the jaguar that I encountered in the vision was the inspiration for the Sphinx of Giza. (I agree with John Anthony West that the Sphinx originally had the head of a lion.) I think the jaguar represents the heart mind, the limbic system, as it appeared in the liminal world, a Leo
